content: Just as human language learners use written reflexion (reflection) in high English curricula to correct persistent grammatical and rhetorical errors by explicitly reviewing past work, large language models can borrow this structured educational reflexion framework to build graded self-reflection mechanisms that iteratively correct output flaws aligned with human communication norms. This framing turns abstract LLM self-reflection into a scaffolded learning process that mirrors how humans intentionally build reflective skill through structured practice.
